exports.content: In Liu et al.'s continuum-model electronic-structure calculation for magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene, adding atomic-registry-driven lattice relaxation produces reconstructed geometry with enlarged AB/BA regions and reduced AA regions; compared with the rigid unreconstructed calculation, this reconstructed geometry reproduces the key STS spectroscopic trends of broader low-energy flat bands and greater energetic isolation from remote bands, while the calculation omits explicit spatially non-uniform heterostrain and therefore does not reproduce extra flat-band peak splitting, such as three-subpeak structure, observed in some spectra [@Liu2020].
